title: Senior Security Engineer (AI & Automation)
description:
**Company Description** **Are you ready to trade your job for a journey? Become a FlyMate!**  

  

Passion, excitement \& global collaboration are all core to what it means to be a FlyMate. At Flywire, we’re on a mission to deliver the world’s most important and complex payments. We use our Flywire Advantage \- the combination of our next\-gen payments platform, proprietary payment network and vertical specific software, to help our clients get paid, and help their customers pay with ease \- no matter where they are in the world.


What more do we need to truly be unstoppable? Perhaps, that is you!

**Who we are:**


Flywire is a global payments enablement and software company, founded more than a decade ago to solve high\-stakes, high\-value payments. We’ve scaled into new regions and industry verticals and expanded our product offerings to deliver meaningful value to our clients around the world.


Today we support more than 5,100 clients across the global education, healthcare, travel \& B2B industries, with diverse payment methods across 240 countries \& territories and more than 140 currencies.


With over 1,400\+ global FlyMates, representing more than 40 nationalities, and in 15 offices world\-wide, we’re looking for FlyMates to join the next stage of our journey as we continue to grow.

 **Job Description** **The Opportunity:**

We are looking for a seasoned, forward\-thinking Senior Security Engineer to join our team in Adelaide, Australia,. We expect this role to be able to blend traditional security architecture with new technologies like AI\-driven automation.


You will lead the charge in evolving our security posture by integrating Large Language Models (LLMs) directly into our CI/CD pipelines. Your mission is to automate repetitive tasks and provide high\-fidelity, automated security reviews at scale, ensuring our development houses move fast while maintaining security of the products.

**️ Key Responsibilities**

* AI\-Driven Security Automation: Design, prompt\-engineer, and deploy automated security review workflows using for example Claude or other LLM APIs to perform real\-time code analysis and architectural reviews within our CI/CD environment.
* Security Design \& Architecture: Lead secure design reviews and advanced threat modeling for our complex payment systems.
* Engineering Collaboration \& Leadership: Act as a technical bridge between Security and Engineering teams. Collaborate frequently with different engineering teams to identify and address security issues.
* Advanced Full\-Stack Reviews: Oversee deep\-dive technical reviews, moving beyond basic scans to perform source code audits and live application testing on high\-risk features.
* Automation \& SDLC: Contribute and take ownership for the automated security controls we are building and take an active part in every aspect of the secure software development lifecycle (S\-SDLC).
* Mentorship \& Guidance: Provide hands\-on remediation guidance and mentor junior security or software engineers, also members of Product teams, on both traditional exploits and emerging AI\-specific vulnerabilities.

  

**Qualifications** **Here’s What We’re Looking For**


Experience \& Mindset

* 5\+ years in Application Security. Proven experience performing web application penetration tests and vulnerability research. Skills in source code auditing, product assessments and interaction with product teams, and also experience with development of security tools are essential.
* Automation First: A passion for replacing manual, repetitive tasks with intelligent, automated scripts and AI workflows.


AI \& LLM Proficiency

* Prompt Engineering for Security: Demonstrated ability to use tools like Claude for security\-specific tasks like code summarization, vulnerability detection, and automated fix generation.
* AI Pipeline Integration: Experience building custom tools or wrappers that leverage LLMs to analyze pull requests and provide context\-aware security feedback.
* OWASP Top 10 for LLMs: Deep practical knowledge of defending against Prompt Injection, Insecure Output Handling, and Model Inversion.


Technical Proficiencies

* Full\-Stack Depth: experience with Python, Ruby on Rails, Java and modern web dev (JavaScript, Node.js, etc.).
* Cloud \& DevOps: Good knowledge of AWS or alike cloud environments, containerization (Docker), and building/maintaining GitLab CI pipelines.
* Security Tooling: Advanced experience with SAST, DAST, and SCA tools, and more importantly, the ability to tune them to eliminate noise.
* Crypto \& Auth: Deep understanding of applied cryptography, OAuth2, SAML, and SSO implementations.


Soft Skills

* Strategic Communication: Ability to translate complex AI\-generated findings into actionable business risks for stakeholders.
* Empathy\-Driven Security: A collaborative approach that treats developers and product teams as partners, focusing on enablement rather than friction.


Compliance \& Standards

* Practical experience aligning technical controls with standards like SOC 1, SOC 2, PCI\-DSS, and emerging AI\-governance frameworks.

 **Additional Information** **What We Offer:**

* Competitive compensation
* Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP)
* Flying Start \- Our immersive Global Induction Program (Meet our Execs \& Global Teams)
* Work with brilliant people that will keep you on your toes, learn more about their journeys by checking out \#InsideFlywire on social media
* Dynamic \& Global Team (we have been collaborating virtually for years!)
* Wellbeing Programs (Mental Health, Wellness, Yoga/Pilates/HIIT Classes) with Global FlyMates
* Be a meaningful part in our success \- every FlyMate makes an impact
* Competitive time off including FlyBetter Days to volunteer in a cause you believe in and
* Digital Disconnect Days!
* Great Talent \& Development Programs (Managers Taking Flight – for new or aspiring managers!)

**Submit today and get started!**


We are excited to get to know you! Throughout our process you can expect to meet with different FlyMates including the Hiring Manager, Peers on the team, the VP of the department, and a skills assessment. Your Talent Acquisition Partner will walk you through the steps and be your “go\-to” person for any questions.

*Flywire is an equal opportunity employer. With over 40 nationalities across 14 different offices diversity, gender equality, and inclusion are at the core of our people agenda. We believe our FlyMates are our greatest asset, and we’re excited to watch our unique culture evolve with each new hire.*  

  

\#Li\-Hybrid

title: UI/UX Designer & Animator
description:
**UI/UX Designer \& Animator — Job Description**

**Summary**

Designs intuitive, accessible user interfaces and engaging motion/animation that enhance user experience, communicate brand, and improve usability across web and mobile products.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Lead user‑centered design: user research, personas, user journeys, wireframes, and interactive prototypes.
* Design high‑fidelity UI visuals, components, and design systems consistent with brand and accessibility guidelines.
* Create motion design and animations (micro‑interactions, transitions, onboarding flows, data visualizations) to improve usability and delight users.
* Produce interactive prototypes for usability testing and stakeholder review using tools like Figma, Sketch, Adobe XD, Principle, or Framer.
* Conduct usability studies, heuristic evaluations, and A/B tests; synthesize findings into actionable design improvements.
* Collaborate with product managers, developers, researchers, and visual designers to define requirements and ensure design feasibility.
* Translate design into implementation: provide specs, motion assets, Lottie/JSON animations, and CSS/JS guidance; review front‑end implementation for fidelity.
* Maintain and evolve component libraries, motion guidelines, and documentation for cross‑team reuse.
* Advocate for accessibility (WCAG) and inclusive design practices; ensure designs meet performance and responsiveness constraints.
* Stay current with UX trends, interaction patterns, and animation techniques; propose new patterns where beneficial.
* Mentor junior designers and animators; participate in design critiques and cross‑functional reviews.

**Required Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree in Interaction Design, Graphic Design, HCI, Animation, or equivalent experience.
* 3\+ years combined experience in UI/UX design and motion/animation for digital products (adjust by level).
* Strong portfolio demonstrating UX process, visual design, and motion/animation work.
* Proficiency with design and prototyping tools (Figma, Sketch, Adobe Creative Suite, After Effects, Principle, Framer).
* Experience creating production‑ready animations (Lottie/Bodymovin, JSON exports) and handing off to developers.
* Solid understanding of HTML/CSS/JS basics and how animations are implemented on the web/mobile.
* Knowledge of accessibility standards (WCAG) and inclusive design practices.
* Strong user research, information architecture, and interaction design skills.
* Excellent communication and collaboration skills.

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Experience with motion design tools (After Effects, Lottie, Rive) and animation systems for mobile (Android, iOS) or web.
* Familiarity with front‑end frameworks (React, Flutter) and animation libraries (Framer Motion, GSAP).
* Experience building or maintaining design systems and component libraries.
* Background in illustration, 3D, or kinetic typography for advanced interaction design.
* Experience with analytics and experimentation (Amplitude, Mixpanel, Optimizely) to measure UX impact.

**Competencies \& Soft Skills**

* Empathy and user advocacy.
* Visual sensibility and attention to motion timing, easing, and choreography.
* Pragmatic approach balancing polish with performance and engineering constraints.
* Strong storytelling and ability to present design rationale to stakeholders.
* Mentorship and collaboration mindset.

Pay: $45\.26 – $78\.47 per hour

Benefits:

* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Life insurance
* Salary packaging

Work Location: On the road

title: Lead Software Engineer - AI & Automation
description:
There are Adelaide roles, and then there are Adelaide roles. This one will not ask you to commute across town during school pickup, explain "what AI is" to a committee of 14 people, or pretend that innovation means putting ChatGPT in a PowerPoint and calling it strategy. You're welcome.
   

  

 First Focus is one of Australia's leading Managed Service Providers, with 330\+ people across Australia, New Zealand, and the Philippines. We help businesses get better outcomes from technology, which sounds very grown\-up, because it is. Mostly.
   

  

 Our
 **AI, Automation \& Adoption team** 
 is a small internal innovation team with a big remit: build practical AI, automation, tooling, agents, integrations, and internal platforms that actually get used. Not innovation theatre. Not a sandbox that gathers dust. Real things, in production, helping real people do real work with slightly fewer spreadsheets. Humanity may survive after all.
   

  

 We're looking for a
 **Lead Software Engineer, AI \& Automation** 
 to become the engineering backbone of the team.
   

  

 You'll take prototypes, AI concepts, and promising ideas from "this might work" to "this is secure, supportable, documented, and nobody is quietly crying in six months". A noble cause.
   

  

**Why this role is worth your attention**
* You'll build AI and automation products that actually get used.
* You'll have real technical ownership, not fake ownership with six approval layers and a steering committee named after a weather event.
* You'll help shape engineering standards for a small, fast\-moving team.
* You'll work with modern AI\-assisted development tools.
* You'll balance greenfield builds with proper sustaining engineering.
* You'll be joining a team that cares about outcomes over optics.


**What you'll be doing**
* Designing, building, testing, deploying, and supporting production\-grade AI and software solutions.
* Taking prototypes and proof\-of\-concepts into stable operational systems.
* Building internal tools, AI agents, automation platforms, integrations, and supporting services.
* Owning the systems the team builds after launch, including maintenance, bug fixes, enhancements, performance, security, stability, and documentation.
* Helping define practical engineering standards across code quality, testing, secure development, documentation, deployment, source control, code review, and AI\-assisted development workflows.
* Reducing technical debt before it becomes a haunted house.
* Working closely with the AI, Automation \& Adoption Manager and business stakeholders to turn ideas into useful, deliverable solutions.
* Providing technical leadership across architecture, planning, and delivery.


**Requirements**
**What you'll bring**
* Strong commercial software engineering experience across modern application stacks.
* Experience delivering and supporting production\-grade applications or services.
* Solid understanding of secure development, CI/CD, testing, source control, deployment, code review, and operational readiness.
* Practical experience using AI\-assisted development tools and workflows.
* Strong problem\-solving skills and a systems\-thinking mindset.
* The ability to work autonomously in fast\-moving and sometimes ambiguous environments.
* Good communication skills with technical and non\-technical stakeholders.
* Enough judgement to know when "move fast" is useful, and when it is just a polite way to create future rubble.


**Nice to have**
* Experience building AI agents or LLM\-powered systems.
* Experience with Claude Code, Codex, Lovable, or similar tools.
* Proficiency with Python, TypeScript, React, APIs, cloud\-native development, or automation tooling.
* Experience turning experimental systems into production platforms.
* Experience defining lightweight engineering governance.
* Exposure to Microsoft 365, Azure, or MSP environments.


 No, we do not expect one person to be world\-class in all of that. That would be silly, and probably expensive enough to make Finance raise an eyebrow.
   

  

**What Success Looks Like**
* Prototypes become stable, supportable platforms.
* AI and automation initiatives deliver measurable operational outcomes.
* The systems you help build stay secure, maintainable, and documented after release.
* Engineering maturity improves without slowing innovation to a crawl.
* Internal teams adopt the tools because they help, not because someone sent a "friendly reminder".
* The team has clearer standards, less rework, and fewer "why did we do it like this?" moments.


**About First Focus**
 First Focus is a stable, growing, people\-first technology business. We have been around long enough to know that good engineering needs curiosity, trust, clarity, and the occasional reality check.
   

  

 We are big on our values, especially
 **Be the Solution** 
 and
 **Never Stop Growing** 
 for this role. You can read more about them here: https://www.firstfocus.com.au/about\-us/
   

  

 That means we like people who ask why, own problems properly, keep learning, share what they know, and do not need a marching band every time they fix something important.
   

  

**Benefits**
* Competitive salary package including up to $170,000 \+ super.
* Free access to Uprise, including 1:1 coaching with qualified psychologists or counsellors.
* Flexible working for work\-life balance.
* "Never Stop Growing": 10 paid study days per year, exam fees paid, and pay rises for certifications, conditions apply.
* Weekly internal training and access to a learning library.
* FastTrack mentoring program.
* MAD, our Making a Difference Council, supporting equality, charity, and environmental initiatives.
* Tesla company car option, conditions apply.
* Regular social events and a genuinely supportive team culture.


**Final bit**
 This is a role for someone who wants to build useful AI and automation products, own them properly, and help shape the standards around them.
   

  

 Someone who likes pace, but not chaos. Someone who can move fast, then still leave the codebase in a state that does not require an apology note.
   

  

 If that sounds like you, apply now. If you're reading this from Adelaide with a Farmers Union in hand and a strong opinion about the Britannia roundabout, even better.
   

  

*First Focus welcomes applications from Neurodiverse candidates. We recommend \& appreciate disclosure along with the application so that a copy of our Neurodiversity Statement can be provided outlining the flexible options we can offer to best showcase your skills.*

title: Senior Full Stack Software Engineer I
description:
**Are you ready to trade your job for a journey? Become a FlyMate!**
  

  

  

 Passion, excitement \& global collaboration are all core to what it means to be a FlyMate. At Flywire, we’re on a mission to deliver the world’s most important and complex payments. We use our Flywire Advantage \- the combination of our next\-gen payments platform, proprietary payment network and vertical specific software, to help our clients get paid, and help their customers pay with ease \- no matter where they are in the world.
   

  





 What more do we need to truly be unstoppable? Perhaps, that is you!
   

  





**Who we are:**




 Flywire is a global payments enablement and software company, founded more than a decade ago to solve high\-stakes, high\-value payments in higher education. We’ve since scaled into new regions and industry verticals and expanded our product offerings to deliver meaningful value to our clients around the world.
   

  





 Today we support more than 5,100 clients across the global education, healthcare, travel \& B2B industries, with diverse payment methods across 240 countries \& territories and more than 140 currencies.
   

  





 With over 1,200 global FlyMates, representing more than 40 nationalities, and in 12 offices world\-wide, we’re looking for FlyMates to join the next stage of our journey as we continue to grow.a
   

  

  

  




**Job Description**

**The Opportunity:**




 We, at Flywire, are looking for an experienced Senior Software Engineer. As a Senior Engineer, you will be a contributor to our billing and payments product that supports the money movement of hundreds of millions every day. This is a
 **full\-stack** 
 role that requires a blend of deep technical proficiency and the ability to navigate a variety of client requirements. You will be expected to work independently most of the time while maintaining a collaborative presence with other teams across the globe. The ideal candidate for this position is a person that thrives in taking on new challenges. You will be joining a small team in Australia that will be in charge of designing new functionality and improving our current core capabilities. We are looking for an engineer who balances a pragmatic design approach with a commitment to building quality, secure, and scalable products.
   

  





 Key Responsibilities
   

  




* Feature Development: Design, build, and maintain high\-quality, testable, and maintainable code within our billing and payments system. You will be expected to work independently at times, making informed technical and architectural decisions while using product goals as your "North Star".
* AI\-Leveraged Exploration: Proactively uses AI tools to analyse existing codebases, answer architectural questions, and accelerate your understanding of complex system interdependencies is a must.
* Quality \& Security: Ensure that security, quality, scaling, and performance are never sacrificed for speed.
* System Diagnostics: Regularly use diagnostic tools to resolve issues and answer technical questions quickly and calmly.
* Stakeholder Engagement: Communicate effectively across engineering and product teams. You should be comfortable joining client meetings to assist in gathering requirements or troubleshooting complex problems.
* Collaborative Problem Solving: Participate in solutioning meetings, comfortably debating the pros and cons without ego.



 Technical Requirements
   

  




* Java Proficiency: Strong, professional experience in Java development is a must.
* Database Knowledge: Deep understanding of relational databases (MySQL) and Object\-Oriented concepts.
* Core Stack (Nice to Have): Experience with Spring Core, Spring MVC, and Hibernate.
* Front\-End Skills: Familiarity with HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and JSP (Java Server Pages).
* Modern Workflow: You must be comfortable using AI\-assisted development tools to enhance productivity and writing comprehensive test cases for all new code.


**Qualifications**

 Here's what we are looking for:
   

  




* Self\-Reliant \& Proactive: You are a "figure\-it\-out" engineer who takes ownership of tasks but knows when to approach leadership with well\-formed, insightful questions.
* Adaptable: You have the aptitude to learn new systems or legacy codebases quickly and can manage your own time effectively to meet deadlines.
* Ambiguity Navigator: You can take high\-level product goals and turn them into actionable technical designs without requiring constant oversight.
* Global Collaborator: You enjoy working with diverse teams across different time zones and possess outstanding verbal and written communication skills.
* 10\+ years of professional experience in application development.
* Proven track record as a Java Developer in a production environment.
* Experience in FinTech or Higher Education sectors is highly appreciated.
* Ability to deliver high\-quality code and adapt to a fast\-paced, evolving environment.


**Additional Information**

**What We Offer:**



* Competitive compensation
* Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP)
* Flying Start \- Our immersive Global Induction Program (Meet our Execs \& Global Teams)
* Work with brilliant people that will keep you on your toes, learn more about their journeys by checking out \#InsideFlywire on social media
* Dynamic \& Global Team (we have been collaborating virtually for years!)
* Wellbeing Programs (Mental Health, Wellness, Yoga/Pilates/HIIT Classes) with Global FlyMates
* Be a meaningful part in our success \- every FlyMate makes an impact
* Competitive time off including FlyBetter Days to volunteer in a cause you believe in and
* Digital Disconnect Days!
* Great Talent \& Development Programs (Managers Taking Flight – for new or aspiring managers!)



**Submit today and get started!**




 We are excited to get to know you! Throughout our process you can expect to meet with different FlyMates including the Hiring Manager, Peers on the team, the VP of the department, and a skills assessment. Your Talent Acquisition Partner will walk you through the steps and be your “go\-to” person for any questions.
   

  





*Flywire is an equal opportunity employer and follows a policy of administering all employment decisions and personnel actions without regard to race, colour, religion, sex, pregnancy, gender identity, national origin, age, ancestry, physical or mental disability, sexual orientation, genetic disposition or carrier status, veteran status, or any other category protected under applicable national, federal, state or local law.*

title: Senior Software Engineer II
description:
**Are you ready to trade your job for a journey? Become a FlyMate!**
  

  

  

 Passion, excitement \& global collaboration are all core to what it means to be a FlyMate. At Flywire, we’re on a mission to deliver the world’s most important and complex payments. We use our Flywire Advantage \- the combination of our next\-gen payments platform, proprietary payment network and vertical specific software, to help our clients get paid, and help their customers pay with ease \- no matter where they are in the world.
   

  





 What more do we need to truly be unstoppable? Perhaps, that is you!
   

  





**Who we are:**




 Flywire is a global payments enablement and software company, founded more than a decade ago to solve high\-stakes, high\-value payments in higher education. We’ve since scaled into new regions and industry verticals and expanded our product offerings to deliver meaningful value to our clients around the world.
   

  





 Today we support more than 5,100 clients across the global education, healthcare, travel \& B2B industries, with diverse payment methods across 240 countries \& territories and more than 140 currencies.
   

  





 With over 1,200 global FlyMates, representing more than 40 nationalities, and in 12 offices world\-wide, we’re looking for FlyMates to join the next stage of our journey as we continue to grow.a
   

  

  

  




**Job Description**

**The Opportunity :**




 We, at Flywire, are looking for a highly experienced Senior Software Engineer II. As a Senior Software Engineer II, you will be a key contributor and technical leader for our billing and payments product that supports the money movement of hundreds of millions every day. This is a full\-stack role that requires a blend of deep technical proficiency, architectural foresight, and the ability to navigate highly complex client requirements. You will be expected to work autonomously, often driving cross\-team technical initiatives, while maintaining a strong, collaborative presence with other teams across the globe. The ideal candidate for this position is a person that thrives in taking on challenges of high scope and complexity. You will be joining a small team in Australia that will be in charge of designing new functionality, improving our current core capabilities, and elevating our engineering practices. We are looking for an engineer who balances a pragmatic, long\-term design approach with a commitment to building highly secure, reliable, and scalable systems.
   

  





 Key Responsibilities
   

  




* Architecture \& Development: Architect services and systems using well\-accepted design patterns to allow for iterative development and future scaling. Working closely with US technical leadership, you will translate global architectural vision into local execution, confidently starting new projects from scratch in high\-risk, high\-volume areas while consistently applying SOLID principles.
* AI\-Augmented Engineering: Design and implement team\-level AI\-augmented workflows that measurably improve velocity for the Australian engineering team. You will evaluate new AI tools (assessing ROI, security, and data privacy), mitigate risks at a systems level, and create reusable configurations that benefit teammates locally and abroad.
* Operations \& Reliability: Drive, design, and deliver software that meets strict reliability, performance, and scalability requirements. You will define SLOs/SLIs, actively work with global security teams to refine organisational strategy, and act as a regional owner for postmortems to ensure long\-term mitigations are executed.
* Complex Problem Solving: Act as the primary technical escalation point in the APAC time zone to identify and solve complex cross\-system problems. You will lead local and cross\-team groups during high\-urgency production issues, correctly assessing scope and impact, and collaborating with global teams for seamless incident handoffs.
* Leadership \& Mentorship: Serve as the Tech Lead on the ground in Australia. You will mentor the local team, structure technical proposals into the global company roadmap, defend team goals, and foster a culture where teammates openly share opinions—using contradictory viewpoints to drive constructive conversations.
* Project Management \& Productivity: Successfully manage the Australian team's commitments, roadmap planning, and delivery in alignment with the global team. You will critically review epics to ensure they are appropriately sized, schedule technical debt relative to global cross\-team changes, and routinely manage multiple major projects simultaneously.
* Strategic Communication: Act as the technical bridge between the Australia office and the global team. You will distill technical feedback from global teams into concrete local proposals, and communicate effectively across Flywire and with external clients, maintaining composure and clarity in high\-pressure discussions.



 Technical Requirements
   

  




* Advanced Java Proficiency: Expert\-level professional experience in Java development, with a deep understanding of how libraries and software evolve over time (often reading vendor libraries to identify undocumented behaviors).
* System Architecture: Deep understanding of relational databases (MySQL), infrastructure tooling, and cross\-system data flows. Expertise in applying common patterns for service reliability (retries, failover, circuit breakers, etc.).
* Core Stack: Extensive experience with Spring Core, Spring MVC, and Hibernate, alongside the ability to evaluate and determine new technologies that align with current usage.
* Front\-End Skills: Familiarity with HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and JSP (Java Server Pages).
* Modern Workflow \& Automation: Ability to drive automation and performance indicators for services, leveraging advanced monitoring tooling and AI\-assisted tools for enterprise\-grade deployment.


**Qualifications**

 Here's what we are looking for :
   

  




* Minimum of 8\+ years of relevant experience
* Strategic \& Visionary: You contribute directly to the organization's engineering strategy and documentation. You evaluate and create new product features in collaboration with the product team while continually simplifying product and technical design.
* Culture Builder: You foster a culture of clear, concise, audience\-oriented communication. You work within your team to instill priority setting and urgency aligned with organizational goals.
* Adaptable: You have the aptitude to learn new systems or legacy codebases quickly and can manage your own time effectively to meet deadlines.
* Cross\-Functional Collaborator: You proactively anticipate problems in cross\-team projects before they require escalation and gladly help other teams unblock design doubts using your deep architectural knowledge.
* Global Collaborator: You enjoy working with diverse teams across different time zones and possess outstanding verbal and written communication skills.
* Professional experience in application development commensurate with the SS2 level (extensive track record as a lead or principal\-level contributor in a complex production environment).
* Proven ability to independently identify, scope, and deliver cross\-team technical solutions.
* Experience in FinTech, Payments, or Higher Education sectors is highly appreciated.
* Demonstrated history of successfully managing multi\-team projects, architectural design, and high\-pressure incident response.


**Additional Information**

**What We Offer:**



* Competitive compensation
* Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP)
* Flying Start \- Our immersive Global Induction Program (Meet our Execs \& Global Teams)
* Work with brilliant people that will keep you on your toes, learn more about their journeys by checking out \#InsideFlywire on social media
* Dynamic \& Global Team (we have been collaborating virtually for years!)
* Wellbeing Programs (Mental Health, Wellness, Yoga/Pilates/HIIT Classes) with Global FlyMates
* Be a meaningful part in our success \- every FlyMate makes an impact
* Competitive time off including FlyBetter Days to volunteer in a cause you believe in and
* Digital Disconnect Days!
* Great Talent \& Development Programs (Managers Taking Flight – for new or aspiring managers!)



**Submit today and get started!**




 We are excited to get to know you! Throughout our process you can expect to meet with different FlyMates including the Hiring Manager, Peers on the team, the VP of the department, and a skills assessment. Your Talent Acquisition Partner will walk you through the steps and be your “go\-to” person for any questions.

title: Full Stack Developer
description:
McMahon Services is seeking an experienced Full Stack Developer to join our internal Application Development team.
 



 This role is an opportunity to work on a major internal transformation program delivering modern applications, integrations and data capabilities that will support the future of our business operations.
 



**About McMahon Services** 




 McMahon Services is one of Australia’s leading privately owned industrial services and construction businesses. We deliver complex projects across civil infrastructure, environmental remediation, demolition, decommissioning, asset management, recycling and disposal for major industry, government and Defence clients across Australia.
 



 Behind the trucks, plant and people sits a growing internal technology capability. We are investing significantly in modern tooling, structured data and AI\-assisted delivery to support how our business operates and scales.
 



**About the Role** 




 This role will work across backend services, APIs, databases and front\-end applications, contributing to the delivery of modern business systems and integrations.
 



 You will collaborate closely with internal stakeholders and subject matter experts, helping shape technical solutions while contributing to architecture, design and continuous improvement initiatives.
 



 This is not a one\-project role, there is a strong pipeline of future initiatives across business systems, reporting, integrations and AI\-enabled solutions.
 



**What You’ll Do** 



* Build end\-to\-end software solutions across backend services, APIs and Angular front\-end applications
* Develop and maintain integrations with ERP and business systems
* Work directly with stakeholders to shape and refine solutions
* Contribute to technical architecture and platform improvement initiatives
* Design and optimise relational databases and reporting data structures
* Participate in iterative delivery, testing and continuous improvement activities
* Contribute to AI\-assisted development and modern engineering practices



**What You’ll Bring** 



* Commercial experience as a Full Stack Developer (Intermediate or Senior level)
* Strong C\# /.NET capability building APIs and backend services
* Experience with relational databases and SQL Server
* Front\-end development experience using Angular or similar SPA frameworks
* Experience with Git and modern development workflows
* Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills
* Ability to work autonomously and solve complex problems



**What Will Set You Apart** 



* Azure experience including App Services, Functions and DevOps
* Experience integrating with ERP systems (Pronto, SAP, Dynamics or similar)
* Exposure to AI/LLM technologies and AI\-assisted development workflows
* Experience with distributed systems, workflow orchestration or observability tooling




**Why Join McMahon Services?** 



* Opportunity to work on a major business transformation program
* Modern technology stack and AI\-enabled development environment
* Strong pipeline of future projects and long\-term growth opportunities
* Small, capable team with real ownership and influence
* Values\-driven culture focused on Reputation, Integrity, Safety and Excellence



 If you’re a developer who wants ownership, modern tools and the opportunity to shape business\-critical systems, we would like to hear from you, apply now!
 



**No recruitment agencies please.** 




*At McMahon Services, we embrace diversity and inclusion as fundamental values. We believe that a diverse workforce fosters innovation, creativity and a deeper understanding of the communities we serve. We are committed to creating an inclusive environment where all employees feel valued, respected and empowered to contribute their unique perspectives and talents. We encourage applications from people of all backgrounds, identities and experiences.*

title: Senior Software Engineer
description:
* Drive stability and uplift across critical operational systems
* Solve complex engineering problems in a diverse technology landscape
* Work autonomously with real impact in a high trust environment

We’re investing in and developing large\-scale renewables and storage projects as part of our ongoing effort to provide our customers with reliable, affordable and sustainable energy.

***Ready to make a real impact?***
 Join a team responsible for sustaining and improving critical operational systems that support core business functions. This is a hands\-on engineering role focused on solving complex issues, improving reliability and delivering practical enhancements across a broad and evolving technology environment.
   

  

***Why you’ll love working here***
 At Alinta Energy, we’re taking real action to deliver reliable and sustainable energy for Australia — and that practical approach runs through everything we do. You’ll get clear direction, genuine flexibility, and the support you need to grow, all while working on projects that make a lasting difference. We’re big enough to create impact, but small enough that your ideas and effort will always be seen.
   

  

 There are plenty of other great benefits of working for Alinta Energy, including flexible working, energy discounts, an Employee Assistance Program (EAP), a paid volunteer day each year, fully stocked kitchens, and lots more.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
* Maintaining stability and reliability across critical operational and integration systems
* Diagnosing complex issues across batch, real time and integration workloads
* Implementing targeted enhancements within an established architecture
* Collaborating with technology teams to deliver pragmatic solutions and improvements
* Supporting incident response and contributing to system resilience and continuity


**Your Experience**
* Extensive hands\-on software engineering experience across mixed technology environments
* Strong capability across languages in C\#/.net, Java, SQL and scripting tools
* Confidence working across legacy and modern systems without full context
* Proven ability to diagnose issues and deliver practical solutions under pressure
* Clear communication skills and a pragmatic, outcome focused mindset


*Please note this role requires full working rights within Australia.*
 This role suits a senior engineer who enjoys getting hands on, solving complex problems and working across diverse systems in a pragmatic and outcome driven environment.
   

  

***Like what you’re seeing?***
 If you like the sound of this, and you’ve got experience in a similar role, we’d love to talk to you. Come and
 *discover better together* 
 with Alinta Energy.
   

  

 We are a 2026 Circle Back Initiative Employer – we commit to respond to every applicant.
   

  

 If your application progresses, we will need to complete background and pre\-employment checks, such as immigration checks, reference checks, criminal background checks, qualification checks, and additional checks for roles related to finance.
   

  

**Our Diversity Commitment**

As an equal opportunity employer, Alinta Energy encourages applications from any qualified person, including First Nations Australians, members of the LGBTQIA\+ community, people of all ages and people living with a disability. We are also proud to be a WORK180 Endorsed Employer and we are committed to advancing women’s careers.
   

  

**We look forward to hearing from you.**
 Click ‘Apply’ to submit your application.

title: Senior .NET and React Developers (EL1)
description:
As a Senior Developer role you will be responsible for the development and maintenance of the Regulator’s Digital ID technology solution.
   

  

 This role plays a critical role in driving the technical excellence of the development process, ensuring the system’s functionality, performance and security meet high standards. You will focus on the design and implementation of changes to existing software components, ensuring the ongoing integrity, performance, and availability of the system, by collaborating with cross\-functional teams.
   

  

**About The Client**
 The client’s Digital ID System is a key component of Australia’s broader digital transformation and is designed to strengthen the growing digital economy in two main ways:
   

  

* It enables secure, convenient, voluntary, and inclusive methods of verifying identity in online transactions across both public and private sectors, while protecting the privacy and security of personal information
* It helps build trust in digital identity services, streamlines access to online services, and supports continued growth of the digital sector


 The Digital ID System regulator is responsible for:
   

  

* accrediting providers of digital identity services
* approving participation in the Australian Government Digital ID System
* conducting compliance monitoring and enforcement activities relating to non\-privacy aspects of the system


**Contract Details**
**Estimated start date:** 
 Wednesday, 01 July 2026
   

  

**Initial contract duration:** 
 12 months
   

  

**Number of extensions: 2**
**Experience level:** 
 Senior \- EL1 equivalent
   

  

**Location of work:** 
 QLD, WA, ACT, VIC, NSW, NT, SA, TAS
   

  

**Working arrangements:** 
 Hybrid. The client has a requirement to attend the office for 40% of your time each week.
   

  

**Security clearance:** 
 Must be an Australian Citizen able to obtain Baseline
   

  

**Duties and Responsibilities:**
* Work closely with product owners, business analysts and other team members to understand current and emerging requirements to design, test and evaluate changes to the solutions
* Participate in planning, defining, and prioritising upcoming work while ensuring technical standards and processes are followed during development and delivery
* Apply problem solving and programming knowledge to write testable and maintainable code
* Conduct analysis and work with others to provide technical feedback and possible solutions to issues
* Contribute to and maintain internal knowledge bases, coding standards, quality control processes, and best practices to ensure the delivery of high\-quality technical solutions across the branch
* Contribute to the continuous improvement of processes and tooling with a focus on using process automation for deployment and release
* Monitor production system performance and trends to identify areas for improvement \- including but not limited to; fixing bugs and resolving issues, maintaining code, applying security patches and updates
* Implement and maintain continuous integration and deployment pipelines, ensuring smooth, reliable and traceable deployment of software updates


**Technical Skills**
 Experience building applications with .NET and React, using JavaScript/TypeScript for front\-end development and C\# for back\-end services. Familiarity with Dynamics 365 integrations is a plus. We value clean code, API development, and collaboration in Agile teams using Azure DevOps for delivery and version control.
   

  

**Criteria**
 The buyer has specified that each candidate must provide a one\-page pitch to address all criteria specified. This is equal to 5000 characters.
   

  

**Requirements**
**Essential criteria**
* Programming/software development: Level 5 (SFIA) Takes technical responsibility across all stages and iterations of software development. Plans and drives software construction activities. Adopts and adapts appropriate software development methods, tools and techniques. Measures and monitors applications of project/team standards for software construction, including software security.
* Technical skills:
* Experience building applications with .NET and React, using JavaScript/TypeScript for front\-end development and C\# for back\-end services. Familiarity with Dynamics 365 integrations is a plus. We value clean code, API development, and collaboration in Agile teams using Azure DevOps for delivery and version control

title: Full Stack Software Engineers (NV2 / PV Security Clearance)
description:
Position Description
   

  

 CGI is seeking experienced Full Stack Software Engineers to join our growing Space, Defence \& Intelligence (SDI) team. This is an opportunity to work on mission\-critical systems that support Australia’s national security and next\-generation space capabilities.
   

  

 As one of the world’s largest independent IT and business consulting services firms, CGI has a long\-standing global presence across the space, defence, and intelligence sectors. We deliver secure, high\-performance solutions across satellite communications, ground systems, and defence platforms.
   

  

 Joining CGI means becoming part of a team working at the forefront of secure software engineering, contributing to projects that have real\-world impact in highly classified and technically complex environments.
   

  

 At CGI, you are more than just an employee you are an owner. Through our share purchase plan, you can directly benefit from the success you help create.
   

  

 Your future duties and responsibilities
   

  

 As a Full Stack Software Engineer, you will contribute to the design, development, and delivery of secure, scalable applications supporting defence and space systems.
   

  

 Key responsibilities include:
   

  

 Designing, developing, and maintaining full stack applications across front\-end and back\-end systems
   

  

 Building secure, high\-performance software solutions for Defence and Space environments
   

  

 Collaborating with cross\-functional teams including systems, network, and Satcom engineers
   

  

 Developing APIs, microservices, and user interfaces to support mission systems
   

  

 Participating in code reviews, testing, and continuous integration/continuous delivery (CI/CD) practices
   

  

 Troubleshooting, debugging, and optimising system performance
   

  

 Ensuring compliance with security, accreditation, and quality standards
   

  

 Contributing to system documentation, architecture design, and technical decision\-making
   

  

**Required Qualifications To Be Successful In This Role**
 Proven experience in full stack software development
   

  

 Proficiency in one or more front\-end frameworks (e.g. React, Angular, Vue)
   

  

 Strong back\-end development experience (e.g. Java, Python, Node.js, .NET)
   

  

 Experience with APIs, microservices architectures, and modern development practices
   

  

 Familiarity with cloud platforms, containerisation (Docker/Kubernetes), or DevSecOps practices (desirable)
   

  

 Strong problem\-solving skills and attention to detail
   

  

 Ability to work effectively in secure, multidisciplinary environments
   

  

 Excellent communication and collaboration skills
   

  

 Active NV2 or PV Security Clearance (mandatory)
   

  

**Together, as owners, let’s turn meaningful insights into action.**
 Life at CGI is rooted in ownership, teamwork, respect and belonging. Here, you’ll reach your full potential because…
   

  

 You are invited to be an owner from day 1 as we work together to bring our Dream to life. That’s why we call ourselves CGI Partners rather than employees. We benefit from our collective success and actively shape our company’s strategy and direction.
   

  

 Your work creates value. You’ll develop innovative solutions and build relationships with teammates and clients while accessing global capabilities to scale your ideas, embrace new opportunities, and benefit from expansive industry and technology expertise.
   

  

 You’ll shape your career by joining a company built to grow and last. You’ll be supported by leaders who care about your health and well\-being and provide you with opportunities to deepen your skills and broaden your horizons.
   

  

 Come join our team—one of the largest IT and business consulting services firms in the world.

title: Web Developer
description:
**We Are Fujitsu** 




 We use technology to make happier lives. We are a global leader in technology and business solutions that transform organisations and the world around us. We have a long heritage of bringing innovation and expertise, continuously working to contribute to the growth of society and our customers.
 



  










  





**About the Role** 




 We are seeking a
 **Web Developer** 
 with a strong foundation in
 **front\-end technologies and Python\-based backend development** 
 to support ongoing enhancements to a core application (TAC Assist App). This short\-term engagement is ideal for someone who can quickly get up to speed, work across the stack, and contribute to delivering improvements in a fast\-paced environment.
 



 You will work closely with technical leads and support the development, enhancement, and deployment of web applications within a
 **cloud\-based Azure environment** 
 .
 



  





  





  





**Responsibilities and Accountabilities** 



* Develop and enhance web application features using HTML, CSS, and React (as required).
* Support backend development using Python, including tools such as Streamlit, SQLAlchemy, Alembic, and pytest.
* Interpret and extend existing (including AI\-assisted generated) code to ensure functionality and maintainability.
* Deploy and manage applications within Microsoft Azure, including:
* Azure App Services
* Azure Container Registry (ACR)
* Azure Blob Storage
* Work with PostgreSQL databases and support data\-related tasks (exposure to Databricks is beneficial).
* Collaborate with the team on debugging, enhancements, and performance improvements.
* Contribute to testing and continuous improvement practices.
* Work within a modern development setup using VS Code, WSL, and Docker.



  





  





  





**Requirements and Experience** 



* Solid experience in
 **web development** 
 with strong understanding of
 **HTML, CSS** 
 , and exposure to
 **React** 
 .
* Working knowledge of
 **Python development** 
 , including
 **Streamlit, SQLAlchemy, Alembic, and pytest** 
 .
* Experience with
 **Microsoft Azure services** 
 (App Services, ACR, Blob Storage preferred).
* Hands\-on experience with
 **PostgreSQL** 
 or similar databases.
* Familiarity with
 **Docker** 
 ,
 **WSL** 
 , and development tools like
 **VS Code** 
 .
* Strong problem\-solving skills and ability to quickly understand existing codebases.
* Ability to work independently and deliver within a
 **short\-term engagement** 
 .
* Exposure to
 **Databricks** 
 or data engineering environments.
* Basic understanding of
 **AI/ML concepts** 
 .
* Experience working in Agile or collaborative teams.



  





  





  





**Why Fujitsu?** 




 We are an organization with a strong set of values and a history of respecting fairness and equality, whilst promoting diversity, equity, and inclusion. We constantly push ourselves to do better and strive to bring together a diverse mix of perspectives and talents in an inclusive environment, where we encourage our people to bring their full selves to work. We call this Be Completely You.
 



  




* **We put people first. We believe in the power of diversity to drive innovation and our AWEI (Australian Workplace Equality Index) Gold Employer status, and Rainbow Tick certification for LGBTI\+ inclusion show that we value an inclusive culture.**
* **We offer tailored career paths across our global organization to support your professional and personal growth.**
* **Our customers trust us. We have an excellent reputation across the region and globally.**
* **Best in\-class reward and recognition programs flexible work, volunteering leave, and more.**
* **We live our values of aspiration, trust, and empathy, all day, every day.**



  





**Commitment to Diversity, Equity and Inclusion** 




 As an inclusive employer, Fujitsu aims to recruit a diverse range of talents to help us achieve our purpose. In line with our diversity, equity, and inclusion strategy, we highly welcome applications from
 **women and gender\-diverse people; Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people** 
 ;
 **Māori and Pacific people; LGBTI\+ people; people with a disability; culturally and linguistically diverse people; veterans, Australian Defence Force (ADF) and emergency responders** 
 . Transgender and gender\-diverse applicants can request a copy of our Frequently Asked Questions to assist with the recruitment journey.
 



  





 If you don't tick every box in this job description, please don't rule yourself out. Research suggests that underrepresented groups tend to only apply if they meet every requirement. We focus on hiring people who value inclusion, collaboration, adaptability, courage, and integrity, rather than ticking boxes so if this resonates with you, then please apply. For more information, please email careersapac@fujitsu.com.
 



  





**Search Firm Representatives – PLEASE READ:** 




 Fujitsu does not accept unsolicited assistance from search firms for employment opportunities. All CVs or resumes submitted by search firms to any employee at our company without a valid written agreement in place for this position will be considered the sole property of our company. No fee will be paid if a candidate is hired by Fujitsu due to an agency referral where no existing agreement is in place with the Fujitsu Talent Acquisition Team. Where agency agreements are in place, introductions must be through engagement by the Fujitsu Talent Acquisition Team.
 



  





**For Security Cleared Roles – PLEASE NOTE:** 




 Due to the inherent requirements of the role, candidates must be Australian Citizens and hold an Australian Federal Government Security Clearance. The role will or will likely involve access to controlled technology and must satisfy additional restrictions and requirements as a consequence of the International Traffic in Arms Regulations and the Export Administration Regulations. As a consequence of these restrictions and requirements, applicants may be adversely impacted if they are not Australian citizens, are dual nationals, hold citizenship from proscribed countries, or are not of Australian national origin. Please refer to Discrimination (Fujitsu Australia Limited) Exemption 2017 (No 1\) Notifiable instrument NI2020\-672 (ACT).

**About Fivecast**  

  

Fivecast is a world\-leading provider of cutting\-edge digital intelligence solutions. We enable the world’s most important public and private organizations to explore masses of data and uncover actionable insights, critical to protecting global communities.  

  

As a growing tech company with over 150 people globally, all team members are provided with the opportunity to directly contribute to our success, participate in the development of new and emerging technologies and grow their career in a challenging and dynamic high\-tech environment. We are headquartered in Adelaide, Australia with teams in Canberra, Virginia USA, and London UK.  

  

**Our Work Environment and Culture**  

  

Our Adelaide based headquarters is a purpose\-built tech hub centrally based in vibrant Hutt Street. We moved into this brand\-new space in early 2023 to allow for continued collaboration, innovation \& growth.  

  

Our space has been specifically engineered for brainstorming, idea generation, random encounters across teams and to suit our casual, relaxed culture. This has been achieved through amazing features, including an arcade room, pool table, fully equipped kitchens with fruit and snacks, barista quality coffee machine, end of trip facilities (showers, lockers, bike storage, etc), as well as comfortable breakout spaces.  

  

Fivecast has a connected work culture with team members taking advantage of our social clubs, local cafes, bars, pubs and sporting facilities for team activities. At Fivecast we understand the importance of a good work\-life balance and support flexible work arrangements.  

  

*Fivecast is an inclusive employer, we value diversity of thought and experience and believe our inclusive culture contributes to our success. We are committed to building a workforce that is representative of the communities in which we live and work.*

title: Software Developers - Expression of Interest
description:
**Title**
 Software Developers \- Expression of Interest
   

  

**About KBR**
**At KBR – We do things that matter.**
 We deliver science, technology and engineering solutions to governments and companies around the world. KBR employs approximately 38,000 people worldwide with customers in more than 80 countries and operations in over 29 countries.
   

  

 KBR is proud to work with its customers across the globe to provide technology, value\-added services, and long\-term operations and maintenance services to ensure consistent delivery with predictable results. At KBR, We Deliver.
   

  

***Think.KBR.com***
**KBR in Australia**
 With over 65 years working on some of Australia’s largest and most complex projects, KBR has unmatched experience supporting the nation’s critical infrastructure, energy transition and national security priorities. KBR has around 2,000 employees throughout Australia, who are focused on delivering innovative technology and engineering solutions for a safer, more secure and sustainable future.
   

  

**Belong, Connect and Grow at KBR**
 At KBR, we are passionate about our people and our Zero Harm culture. These inform all that we do and are at the heart of our commitment to, and ongoing journey toward being a People First company. That commitment is central to our team of team’s philosophy and fosters an environment where everyone can Belong, Connect and Grow. We Deliver – Together.
   

  

**The Opportunity**
 We are seeking talented, motivated professionals to join our new Modelling and Simulation project dedicated to setting up and integrating the software systems that underpin integrated simulation, test and evaluation (T\&E) environments, including digital twins. This is a hands‑on and high‑impact initiative that will directly influence how our organisation approaches the integration, operation and evolution of complex T\&E capabilities.
   

  

 As a Software Developer you will play a key role in standing up, integrating and operating the software systems that make up our integrated simulation and T\&E environment. You will help bring together a range of modelling, simulation, and test tools into a coherent, working capability and support the engineers who use it. We encourage applications from strong software engineers with broad technical capability, relevant experience, and a commitment to continual learning.
   

  

**This is a permanent full\-time opportunity and can be based in either Adelaide, Canberra, Melbourne, Sydney or Brisbane.**
**The successful candidate must be eligible to hold an NV1 AGSVA security clearance.**
**Responsibilities**
* Set up, integrate, and configure complex software systems that form part of the integrated simulation and T\&E environment.
* Customise and extend off\-the\-shelf and bespoke tools so they work together as a coherent capability.
* Maintain, operate, and support the T\&E environment, including troubleshooting and resolving integration issues.
* Build demonstrators and prototypes to validate integration approaches and exercise the environment.
* Work collaboratively within a small, hands\-on team to deliver high\-quality software and integrations.
* Develop and operate within a modern software environment using contemporary DevSecOps and Git\-driven workflows.
* Contribute to design and integration discussions with practical implementation insight.
* Document configurations, integrations, and code, and share knowledge effectively.
* Continuously learn and adapt as requirements evolve.


**Qualifications, Skills And Experience**
 As the ideal candidate you will bring:
   

  

* Solid software engineering fundamentals with a track record of delivering in complex, production environments
* Proficiency in one or more modern programming languages (e.g. Go, TypeScript, Java, Python, C\#, Rust, or similar), with a demonstrated ability to pick up new languages and tools quickly
* Strong grasp of software design principles, testing, and code quality practices
* Experience integrating, configuring, and operating complex multi\-component software systems
* Understanding of DevSecOps principles, including CI/CD pipelines, automated testing, and security\-in\-the\-pipeline practices
* Proficient with Git\-based workflows and collaborative development practices.


**Desirable**
* Experience with Model\-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) tools such as Cameo
* Background in modelling, simulation, or test and evaluation environments (including digital twins)
* Experience with containerisation (e.g. Docker) and general infrastructure\-as\-code concepts


**Benefits Of KBR**
* A workplace culture certified as a Great Place To Work (Aus, India, UK \& US)
* Flexible working conditions
* Competitive salary (including annual reviews)
* Paid Parental leave
* Paid Reservist leave
* Income protection
* Corporate rewards
* Salary packaging/Novated leasing
* Discounted employee stock purchase plans
* Flu shots, skin checks and private health insurance discounts
* Career development: Online learning, mentorship and career pathways


**If you’re ready to shape tomorrow, let’s get started. Apply Now!**
*KBR acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of Country throughout Australia and their continuing connections to land, sea, community and culture. We pay our respects to Elders past and present.*
*As a Major Service Provider of the Australian Defence Force, an AGSVA security clearance will be required and compliance to International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR). As such, our hiring decisions are based on the key requirements of each role and candidates are selected based on their unique strengths and experiences.*
